---
title: 'History of Church Leaders'
date: '2020-09-10T03:41:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: '(My Journal) Fed By Faith'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '3838478539444199985'
blogger_filename: '/2020/09/history-of-church-leaders.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/(My Journal) Fed By Faith/feed.atom'
---

<p>Church History is fascinating. Imperfect Men and Women creating change for God. You never know what chain of events might be set by your actions. There is a distinct chain of dominoes that fall from Martin Luther's 1517 Theses to Tent Revival Meetings of the 20th Century. Here are just a few of the larger dominoes, following the 10,000s around them would be impossible in a post.&nbsp;</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fractal" target="_blank">Factals</a> are among the most accurate representation of the growth of The Church as it continues to move throughout time. Great <a href="https://fractalfoundation.org/videos/" target="_blank">videos on Fractals</a> at Fractal Foundation Org.</p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/fractalfoundation-wolfesongs-com/sierpinski-zoom41-46bdaf23b592.gif" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="160" data-original-width="185" src="/images/imported/fractalfoundation-wolfesongs-com/sierpinski-zoom41-46bdaf23b592.gif" /></a></div><br /><br /><br /><br /><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romanesco_broccoli">Romanesco broccoli</a>, showing <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Self-similar">self-similar</a> form approximating a natural fractal<div><br /><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/upload-wikimedia-org/fractal-broccoli-138e4c0907c7.jpg" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="600" data-original-width="800" src="/images/imported/upload-wikimedia-org/fractal-broccoli-138e4c0907c7.jpg" /></a></div><p><br /></p><p>*******************************************************</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martin_Luther" target="_blank">Martin</a> Luther (1483-1546) (Lutherism) nailed his 95 Theses to the wall of the church in 1517, essentially lighting the fires of the Protestant Reformation that would lead the church out of the dark ages into the light. He wasn't right or perfect, but he had the first glimmer of light. He laid the foundation of "Saved by Faith" not works.</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Calvinism" target="_blank">John</a> Calvin (1509-1564) (Calvanism) was another stepping stone in the development of Church Theology as the Protestant Reformation came into fruition in the 1400-1600s. He laid the foundation of asking good questions about what it means to be saved.</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arminianism" target="_blank">Jacobus</a> Arminius (1560-1609) (Arminianism) corrected Calvin's obvious errors, but building upon the foundation he laid. He corrected the over corrections, leading us to see how Free Will correctly played a role in salvation.&nbsp;</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Wesley" target="_blank">John </a>Wesley (Methodist 1703-1791), himself an avid preacher of Arminianism, laid further ground work for a second experience after salvation (which he called holiness or sanctification.</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Charles_Fox_Parham" target="_blank">Charles </a>Fox Parham (1873-1929) a Holiness Wesleyan preacher, laid the foundation for a Theological position on subsequent experience, calling it Pentecostalism.&nbsp;</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/William_J._Seymour" target="_blank">William </a>J Seymour (1870-1922) attended one of Parham's trainings, and went on to ignite the world in the Azusa Street Revival (~1906-1913), which eventually birthed Pentecostalism into the world at large, and directly affected the earliest Pentecostal Denominations.</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aimee_Semple_McPherson" target="_blank">Aimee </a>Semple McPherson (1890-1944) affected indirectly by both the happenings of these men in the USA and the revivals overseas in Ireland and <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1904%E2%80%931905_Welsh_revival" target="_blank">Wales</a>, went on to become one of the most powerful preachers (man or woman) of the 20th century.&nbsp;</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_du_Plessis" target="_blank">David du Plessis </a>(1905-1987) Founder of the Charismatic Movement, attempts to reconcile the various groups and beliefs into one experience.&nbsp;</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oral_Roberts#Personal_life" target="_blank">Granville</a> Oral Roberts (1918-2009)&nbsp;</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kenneth_Copeland" target="_blank">Kenneth</a> Copeland (1936-Present)</p><p><a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jack_W._Hayford" target="_blank">Jack</a>&nbsp;Williams Hayford (1934-Present)</p><p><a href="https://gatewaypeople.com/staff/robert-morris" target="_blank">Robert</a> Morris (<a href="https://oneworldinformation.com/pastor-robert-morris/" target="_blank">1961</a>- Present), <a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gateway_Church_(Texas)" target="_blank">Gateway Church</a>.</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/upload-wikimedia-org/fractal-broccoli-138e4c0907c7.jpg" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" data-original-height="600" data-original-width="800" src="/images/imported/upload-wikimedia-org/fractal-broccoli-138e4c0907c7.jpg" /></a></div><br /></div>
